package repository

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for gorm_model_hook_contains_external_io: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Hooks that call HTTP clients, queues, file I/O, or external side effects.
type CaseGormModelHookContainsExternalIoDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseGormModelHookContainsExternalIoModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseGormModelHookContainsExternalIoRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseGormModelHookContainsExternalIoModel
}

type CaseGormModelHookContainsExternalIoService struct {
    repo *CaseGormModelHookContainsExternalIoRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseGormModelHookContainsExternalIoAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveGormModelHookContainsExternalIo(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseGormModelHookContainsExternalIoDTO) error {
    var model CaseGormModelHookContainsExternalIoModel
    db.Where("id = ?", input).First(&model)
    c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"data": model})
    return nil
}

func (m *CaseGormModelHookContainsExternalIoModel) AfterCreate(tx *gorm.DB) error {
    http.Get("https://example.com/webhook?entity_id=" + m.ID)
    return nil
}
